No Poverty.reason: The course syllabus contains no explicit or implicit content related to poverty eradication, economic resources for the poor, or social protection systems.

Zero Hunger.reason: There is no mention of food security, nutrition, sustainable agriculture, or related topics in the course description or learning outcomes.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: The course content does not address health services, disease prevention, mental health, or well-being in any capacity.

Quality Education.reason: The course is tertiary education (4.3), develops critical thinking and academic skills for employment (4.4), and enhances literacy/communication (4.6). It fosters knowledge and skills for sustainable development (4.7).
evidence: This course is designed to encourage students’ exploration of various logical reasoning skills and critical appreciation
Skills acquired in this course will be largely transferrable and applicable outside of the classroom or after graduation
indispensable skill for most professional careers in our times

Gender Equality.reason: The syllabus does not contain any specific content, objectives, or activities related to gender equality, women's empowerment, or addressing gender-based discrimination.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: There is no mention of water resources, sanitation, hygiene, or related environmental issues in the course material.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: The course syllabus does not include any topics or discussions related to energy access, renewable energy, or energy efficiency.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course explicitly states it provides "indispensable" and "transferrable" skills for "most professional careers," directly supporting decent work and employment (Targets 8.5, 8.6).
evidence: Skills acquired in this course will be largely transferrable and applicable outside of the classroom or after graduation
an indispensable skill for most professional careers in our times

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course includes "Using the Computer in Your Research" and emphasizes "the significance of research," offering a minimal, inferred link to enhancing scientific research (Target 9.5).
evidence: Using the Computer in Your Research
the significance of research within a specific discipline

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The syllabus lacks any content or objectives related to reducing inequalities based on age, sex, disability, race, ethnicity, or economic status.

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: There is no mention of urban planning, housing, transport, cultural heritage, or disaster resilience in the course description.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: The course does not cover sustainable resource management, waste reduction, or promoting sustainable consumption and production patterns.

Climate Action.reason: The syllabus contains no content related to climate change mitigation, adaptation, or awareness-raising.

Life Below Water.reason: There is no mention of marine ecosystems, ocean health, pollution, or sustainable fisheries in the course material.

Life on Land.reason: The course does not address terrestrial ecosystems, biodiversity, deforestation, or land degradation.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course cultivates "critical appreciation" and "independent and critical judgment," which are foundational skills for fostering accountable discourse and effective, transparent institutions (Target 16.6).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: While "peer editing" and "group discussion" involve collaboration, they represent a highly localized, minimal form of partnership, not aligning with the global scope of SDG 17.
evidence: engage in serious and strenuous peer editing
group discussion on the assigned articles
